back_translation: And the sons of Israel came, the whole congregation, to the wilderness of Zin in the first month; and the people dwelt in Kadesh; and Miriam died there and was buried there.
And there was no water for the congregation, and they gathered against Moses and against Aaron.
And the people quarreled with Moses, and they said, saying: 'And if only we had perished with the perishing of our brothers before the LORD!'
'And why have you brought the assembly of the LORD into this wilderness, to die there — we and our livestock?'
'And why have you brought us up from Egypt to bring us to this evil place? It is not a place of seed and fig and vine and pomegranate, and there is no water to drink.'
And Moses and Aaron came from before the assembly to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and they fell on their faces; and the glory of the LORD appeared to them.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

'Take the staff, and gather the congregation, you and Aaron your brother, and you shall speak to the rock before their eyes, and it shall give its water; and you shall bring out for them water from the rock, and give drink to the congregation and their livestock.'
And Moses took the staff from before the LORD, just as He commanded him.
And Moses and Aaron gathered the assembly to the face of the rock, and he said to them: 'Hear now, you rebels! From this rock shall we bring out for you water?'
And Moses raised his hand and struck the rock with his staff twice; and much water came out, and the congregation and their livestock drank.

And the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron: 'Because you did not believe in Me, to make Me holy in the eyes of the sons of Israel, therefore you shall not bring this assembly to the land which I have given to them.'
These are the waters of Meribah, where the sons of Israel quarreled with the LORD, and He showed Himself holy among them.
And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom: 'Thus says your brother Israel: You know all the hardship that has found us;'
'And our fathers went down to Egypt, and we dwelt in Egypt many days; and the Egyptians did evil to us and to our fathers;'
'And we cried out to the LORD, and He heard our voice, and sent a messenger, and brought us out from Egypt; and behold, we are in Kadesh, a city at the edge of your border.'

'Let us please pass through your land; we will not pass through field or vineyard, and we will not drink water of a well; we will go by the King's Highway; we will not turn right or left until we have passed through your border.'
And Edom said to him: 'You shall not pass through me, lest with the sword I come out to meet you.'
And the sons of Israel said to him: 'On the main highway we will go up, and if we drink your waters — I and my livestock — I will give their price; only — no matter — on my feet let me pass through.'
And he said: 'You shall not pass through.' And Edom came out to meet him with a large army and with a strong hand.
And Edom refused to give Israel passage through his border; and Israel turned away from him.
And they journeyed from Kadesh, and the sons of Israel came, the whole congregation, to Mount Hor.
And the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron at Mount Hor, at the border of the land of Edom, saying:
'Aaron shall be gathered to his people; for he shall not come to the land which I have given to the sons of Israel, because you rebelled against My mouth at the waters of Meribah.'
'Take Aaron and Eleazar his son, and bring them up Mount Hor.'
'And strip Aaron of his garments, and clothe them on Eleazar his son; and Aaron shall be gathered and shall die there.'
And Moses did as the LORD commanded; and they went up Mount Hor in the sight of the whole congregation.
And Moses stripped Aaron of his garments, and clothed them on Eleazar his son; and Aaron died there on top of the mountain; and Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.
And the whole congregation saw that Aaron had perished, and they wept for Aaron thirty days — all the house of Israel.
